title: Fix: tmux spawn should not use --format json
description:
## Problem

When spawning in tmux mode, the agent runs with `--format json` which outputs raw JSON instead of OpenCode's interactive TUI.

## Current Behavior
```
opencode run --attach ... --format json --title ... "prompt"
# Outputs raw JSON lines to terminal
```

## Expected Behavior
```
opencode run --attach ... --title ... "prompt"
# Shows OpenCode's normal TUI
```

## Root Cause
`pkg/tmux/tmux.go:BuildSpawnCommand()` hardcodes `--format json`.

## Fix
- Remove `--format json` from tmux spawn command
- Keep `--format json` only for inline mode (needed to parse session ID)

## Files
- pkg/tmux/tmux.go line ~10

title: Add tmux integration to orch-go spawn
description:
## Problem

orch-go spawn blocks until the agent session completes because it runs `opencode run --attach` inline. This breaks the orchestration workflow where spawning should be fire-and-forget.

## Current Behavior
```
$ orch-go spawn investigation "task"
[blocks for 5-30 minutes until agent finishes]
```

## Expected Behavior
```
$ orch-go spawn investigation "task"
Spawned: og-inv-task-19dec
   Window: workers-orch-go:5
   Session: ses_abc123
$ [returns immediately, agent runs in tmux window]
```

## Solution

Add tmux integration matching Python orch spawn behavior:

1. Create/find `workers-{project}` tmux session
2. Create new window with agent name
3. Run `opencode run --attach` inside that window
4. Return immediately to caller

## Reference Implementation

Python orch does this via:
- `src/orch/tmuxinator.py` - per-project session management
- `src/orch/spawn.py` - window creation and command execution

## Key Integration Points

- `~/.tmuxinator/workers-{project}.yml` - auto-generated session configs
- Window naming: `🔬 worker: {workspace-name}`
- Pinned `servers` window (window 1) preserved
- Right Ghostty auto-switches to project's workers session

## Deliverables

- `pkg/tmux/` - tmux session/window management
- Update `cmd/orch/spawn` to use tmux wrapper
- Preserve current behavior via `--inline` flag (for CI/headless)

## Validation

- `orch-go spawn investigation "test"` returns immediately
- Agent visible in `workers-{project}` tmux session
- `orch-go status` shows running agent
- Desktop notification on completion

## Context

- Decision: orch-cli/.kb/decisions/2025-12-18-sdk-based-agent-management.md
- Window setup: orch-knowledge/.orch/docs/orchestration-window-setup.md

title: Epic: Go orch-cli Phase 1 - Core OpenCode Client
description:
Rewrite orch-cli core in Go as HTTP client for OpenCode API.

## Context
Decision: .kb/decisions/2025-12-18-sdk-based-agent-management.md
POC validated: ~/Documents/personal/orch-go (spawn, Q&A, SSE, event logging work)

## Goals
- Single Go binary for agent lifecycle (spawn, monitor, complete)
- SSE-based completion detection with desktop notifications
- Native Q&A on completed sessions
- Event logging for observability

## Success Criteria
- orch spawn creates OpenCode session, returns session ID
- orch status shows running sessions from SSE
- orch complete verifies + closes beads issue
- Desktop notification on agent completion
- All events logged to ~/.orch/events.jsonl

## Out of Scope (Phase 2+)
- beads-ui integration
- Full command parity with Python orch
- Daemon mode

## Technical Approach
- Wrap opencode run --attach for spawn/Q&A (validated in POC)
- Direct SSE HTTP client for monitoring
- Shell out to bd CLI for beads operations

title: Go client: OpenCode session management
description:
Build Go package for OpenCode API operations.

## Scope
- Session CRUD (create, get, list, delete)
- Message sending (spawn prompt, Q&A)
- Wrap opencode run --attach --format json

## Implementation
From POC (~/Documents/personal/orch-go/main.go):
- BuildSpawnCommand() - opencode run --attach with prompt
- BuildAskCommand() - opencode run --session for Q&A
- ExtractSessionID() - parse sessionID from JSON events

## Deliverables
- pkg/opencode/client.go - OpenCode client
- pkg/opencode/types.go - Event types, session structs
- Unit tests for parsing

## Validation
- go test passes
- Manual: spawn session, verify session ID returned

title: Go client: SSE event monitoring
description:
Build SSE client for real-time OpenCode event monitoring.

## Scope
- Connect to GET /event SSE stream
- Parse event types: session.created, session.status, message.updated, step_finish
- Detect completion: session.status busy→idle transition
- Trigger callbacks on events

## Implementation
From POC:
- SSEClient struct with URL
- ParseSSEEvent() - extract data: lines
- Event channel for async consumption

## Key Events to Handle
- session.status {busy|idle} - completion detection
- session.created - new session tracking
- step_finish - cost/token tracking

## Deliverables
- pkg/opencode/sse.go - SSE client
- pkg/opencode/events.go - event type definitions
- Integration test with mock SSE server

## Validation
- Connect to running OpenCode, receive events
- Detect session completion correctly

title: Go CLI: orch spawn command
description:
Implement orch spawn in Go.

## Scope
- Parse skill name and task from args
- Load skill content from ~/.claude/skills/
- Create SPAWN_CONTEXT.md in workspace
- Create OpenCode session with prompt
- Register agent in beads (bd create or --issue)
- Return session ID / workspace name

## Dependencies
- orch-go-ph1.1 (OpenCode session management)

- src/orch/spawn.py - SpawnConfig, spawn_with_skill()
- src/orch/spawn_commands.py - CLI interface
- src/orch/spawn_prompt.py - SPAWN_CONTEXT.md generation

## Deliverables
- cmd/orch/spawn.go - spawn command
- pkg/spawn/context.go - SPAWN_CONTEXT.md generation
- pkg/skills/loader.go - skill discovery

- SPAWN_CONTEXT.md written to workspace
- Session ID printed to stdout

title: Go CLI: orch status command
description:
Implement orch status in Go.

## Scope
- List active OpenCode sessions
- Show session state (running/idle)
- Show beads issue linkage
- Format output for terminal

## Dependencies
- orch-go-ph1.1 (OpenCode session management)
- orch-go-ph1.2 (SSE for live status)

## Current Python Reference
- src/orch/monitoring_commands.py - status command
- src/orch/registry.py - agent registry

## Key Difference from Python
- No local registry file needed
- Query OpenCode API directly for session list
- Cross-reference with beads for issue context

## Deliverables
- cmd/orch/status.go - status command
- Table output with: session ID, workspace, status, beads issue

## Validation
- Shows running sessions
- Status updates when session completes

title: Go CLI: orch complete command
description:
Implement orch complete in Go.

## Scope

- Check deliverables exist
- Run verification if specified
- Close beads issue with summary
- Clean up session (optional)

## Dependencies
- orch-go-ph1.1 (OpenCode session management)

## Current Python Reference
- src/orch/complete.py - verification logic
- src/orch/end_commands.py - completion workflow

## Beads Integration
- bd show <id> - get issue details
- bd comment <id> - check for Phase: Complete
- bd close <id> --reason - close on success

## Deliverables
- cmd/orch/complete.go - complete command
- pkg/verify/check.go - verification helpers

- Completes agent that reported Phase: Complete
- Fails for incomplete agents with clear error
- Closes beads issue on success

title: Go: Event logging to events.jsonl
description:
Log all agent lifecycle events to JSONL file.

## Scope

- Event types: spawn, status change, completion, error
- Include timestamp, session ID, metadata

## Dependencies
- orch-go-ph1.2 (SSE events as source)

## Implementation
From POC:
- LogEvent() - append JSON line to file
- Event struct with type, session_id, timestamp, data

## Event Schema
{
  "type": "session.spawned|session.completed|session.error",
  "session_id": "ses_xxx",
  "timestamp": 1703001600,
  "data": {...}
}

## Deliverables
- pkg/events/log.go - event logging
- Rotation/cleanup strategy (optional, defer to Phase 2)

## Validation
- Spawn + complete agent
- events.jsonl contains spawn and completion events

title: Go CLI: Project scaffolding and build
description:
Set up Go project structure and build pipeline.

## Scope
- Initialize Go module
- Set up directory structure (cmd/, pkg/, internal/)
- Makefile or build script
- CI/CD for releases (goreleaser)

## Directory Structure
orch-go/
├── cmd/orch/          # CLI entry points
│   ├── main.go
│   ├── spawn.go
│   ├── status.go
│   └── ...
├── pkg/               # Public packages
│   ├── opencode/      # OpenCode client
│   ├── spawn/         # Spawn logic
│   ├── skills/        # Skill loader
│   └── events/        # Event logging
├── internal/          # Private packages
├── go.mod
├── go.sum
├── Makefile
└── README.md

## Dependencies
- github.com/spf13/cobra (CLI framework)
- github.com/gen2brain/beeep (notifications)

## Deliverables
- Working go build producing orch-go binary
- make install copies to ~/bin/orch (or similar)

## Validation
- go build succeeds
- ./orch-go --help shows commands
